_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
clinical_indications: Simple cystitis/ single episode of Lower UTI
Symptoms like fever, chills, pain renal angle, etc.

claim.label: Prescribed medications including culture sensitive antibiotics
Ultrasound KUB report (if indicated and available)

ppd_preauth.q: Are clinical notes detailing past history of UTI, risk factors, signs and symptoms, and meeting suggestive admission criteria available?
Is planned line of management documented?
Is a photograph of the patient on bed available?

cpd_claim.q: Do the documents (clinical notes and physical examination reports) available detail the need for admission (admission criteria)?
Was there documentary evidence of Urine culture/ sensitivity, Ultrasound KUB (if indicated – like no response to antibiotics in 48 hours and if device available)?
If the patient is an Infant/Child, is abnormal Ultrasound KUB (if indicated) documented in the indoor patient record/discharge summary?
Is there an evidence of temperature being monitored regularly?
Is there a documentary evidence of medication/ treatment given?

Do the discharge documents show the reasons for discharge / referral criteria (secondary to tertiary); counselling on control of risk factors like diabetes/ water intake, etc.; post discharge treatment advise including follow-up after discharge?
If admitted to HDU/ICU, do documents show a need for admission to HDU/ICU?
If admitted to HDU/ICU, is there documentary evidence of monitoring in HDU/ICU?

common_queries: Clinical notes are incomplete or do not justify admission based on past history, risk factors, signs/symptoms, or suggestive admission criteria.
Photograph of the patient on bed is missing at pre-authorization.
Urine culture/sensitivity report or Ultrasound KUB report (if indicated) is missing at claim submission.
There is no evidence of regular temperature monitoring or medication/treatment given during admission.
Admission to HDU/ICU or monitoring within HDU/ICU is not justified by documentation.
Simple cystitis or single episode of Lower UTI is admitted without clear indication or justification.

additional_information.min_doctor_qualification_desirable: MD/ DNB/ equivalent (Medicine) / MD/ DNB/ PG Diploma/ equivalent (Pediatrics)
clinical_key_pointers: Proceed for admission for management of Urinary Tract Infection only if indicated
The diagnosis made should be backed by clinical signs, symptoms, physical examination, investigations.
Look out for Red flag signs (Refer para 1.3 below). If applicable, refer to higher centre for further evaluation and management.
additional_information.comorbidity_management: Counselling on control of risk factors like diabetes/ water intake, etc. is required in discharge documents.
referral_criteria: All males with UTI (except simple cystitis).
Pyelonephritis or complicated UTI not managed in primary care.
Infants and Upper UTI (Pyelonephritis) in children.
contraindications: Nitrofurantoin: Avoid if GFR <45, caution in elderly.
TMP/SMX for pregnant UTI: Avoid in 1st trimester & near term.
